assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup receivable/payable, so the exposure considered is a monetary item rather than a forecast transaction.
The receivable/payable is between group entities with different functional currencies and gives rise to foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Any hedge designation would target only the foreign currency risk component and would meet the formal documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable can be considered under the fair value hedge model because it is a recognised monetary asset or liability exposed to foreign currency risk affecting consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The same recognised intragroup monetary item can also be considered under the cash flow hedge model because the foreign currency risk creates variability in the functional-currency settlement cash flows that can affect consolidated profit or loss.
